events.description: Learn to mix, shake, and enjoy your own handcrafted, seaso nally inspired cocktails! Upon arrival, we will greet you with a glass of sparkling rosé or brut. Sit back and sip while your instructor explains the bartender's tools of the trade and how to use them. Then, get your cameraphone ready, as you will be expertly guided through two shaken-or-stirred recipes to ensure a delicious final product. events.title: Moose on the Loose Kid #4
events.description: Sept 12 - Utah Olympic Park - Moose on the Loose Race Finale Join us for Moose on the Loose 2026, a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are June 13, July 18, August 15, and September 12, with all races beginning at 9:00 AM. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....

events.title: Women's Epic 10K at Deer Valley
events.description: Women's Epic Race at Deer Valley Resort is a 10k loop with a 1335ft vertical trek to the peak and back down. You can hike, jog, or run your journey through gorgeous trees with changing fall leaves and steep climbs. You will undoubtedly come off the mountain with more confidence and grit after completing this race to the top of the world-class Deer Valley Resort located in Park City Utah. And when you get to the finish line you can grab a drink and celebrate!
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: The Freaky 5K
events.location: Midway, UT

events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Art Summer Camps 2026
events.description: Fill your summer with creativity at the Kimball Art Center! Explore the wide world of art in our week-long summer camp. From painting and poetry to mosaics and film, all camps are led by passionate instructors and carefully planned for each age group. Mini Artist – Ages 4-5 Kids – Ages 6-12 Tweens – Ages 10-14 Teens – 13-18 See all camps and summer schedule at kimballartcenter.org/camps/

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....

events.title: The Pendry Plaza
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Tiny Tire Showdown | Balance Bike Race Series
events.description: Tiny bikes, big smiles, and some friendly competition – Basin Recreation’s BRAND NEW Tiny Tire Showdown is coming in hot! Designed for kids ages 4 and under, this exciting 3-race balance bike series is the perfect way for little riders to experience the thrill of racing in a fun and supportive environment. The Tiny Tire Showdown is more than just a race series — it’s a chance for young kids to get involved in the biking community, meet other families, and celebrate the adventurous mountain-town spirit that makes our community so special. From first rides to fast laps, the Tiny Tire Showdown is all about helping young riders build confidence and have fun on two wheels. RACE DAY SCHEDULE 3pm: racer check-in, race plate pickup\*, and course preview 4pm: 2 & under heats begin, followed by 3-4 age group Awards ceremony to follow Helmet and closed toed shoes are required. Traditional balance bikes only: no pedals or e-bikes allowed. Sign up for one race or the full series! Willow Creek Park: Tuesday June 30, 3-6pm Matt Knoop Park: Tuesday July 21, 3-6 pm Trailside Bike Park: Saturday August 8, 3-6 pm \(in conjunction with the Downhill & Grill\) FREE REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED \*Race plates will be picked up at check-in before their first race and used throughout the series....

events.title: herbs for kids
events.description: herbs for kids Tuesday, June 30th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required This class offers information about how to use herbs to keep kids healthy. Merry offers tips on incorporating herbs into their diet as well as healthcare for their nutritional and therapeutic values. She will bring herb cuttings, essential oils, and salves to help demonstrate the healing potential of herbs. Bring a pen and paper to take notes. Merry attended the Southwest School of Botanical Medicine, where Master Herbalist Michael Moore was her teacher. He taught about wild herbs as well as herbs of commerce. Merry had an herbal clinical practice for 20 years \(now retired\). She is a teacher, published author, ethnobotanical researcher, and wild guide. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Jylian Gustlin + Lola: Exhibition
events.description: Gallery MAR is pleased to present a summer exhibition featuring new work by Lola and Jylian Gustlin, opening **July 3rd** with an artist reception from **6–9 pm** at the gallery’s Main Street location in Park City. On view for **three weeks**, the exhibition brings together two distinctive contemporary artists whose practices explore abstraction through color, material, and perception. Lola, a self-taught visual artist based in the San Francisco Bay Area, is best known for her intensely vibrant abstractions created with tinted epoxy resin. Working on a raised horizontal surface, she allows the resin to flow, pool, and spill over the edges of the panel, creating luminous compositions framed by gravity-defying drips that have become a signature of her work. Her newest series, *Quiet Places*, marks an evolution in her practice—moving away from densely layered forms toward simpler shapes, softer palettes, and shadowy overlays that invite viewers to experience moments of calm and serenity. For Lola, color remains the central language of emotion, and each work is intended to create an experience that allows viewers to find their own meaning and sense of optimism within the composition. In conversation with Lola’s emotive abstractions are the richly layered paintings of Jylian Gustlin. Influenced by the San Francisco Bay Area Figurative painters and informed by her background in computer science and digital graphics programming, Gustlin’s work explores the intersection of art, mathematics, and perception. Drawing on concepts such as the Fibonacci sequence and entropy, she creates complex compositions using oil, acrylic, charcoal, wax, gold leaf, pastel, and graphite. Gustlin builds her surfaces through drawing, painting, and scratching into the material itself, resulting in paintings that feel almost sculptural—dynamic fields of gesture, symbol, and color that encourage viewers to look closely and discover new relationships within the work. Together, Lola and Gustlin...
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Queen Nation - Tribute to the Music of Queen at Forum Fest At Canyons VIllage
events.description: Queen Nation, a tribute to Queen, was formed in 2004. Queen Nation consists of: Gregory Finsley on vocals & keyboards as Freddie Mercury, Mike McManus on guitar as Brian May, Pete Burke on drums as Roger Taylor, and Parker Combs on bass as John Deacon. The mission of the show is to carry on the musical torch and pay homage to the golden age of vintage Queen concerts. Queen Nation’s live 90-minute production of Queen’s greatest hits preserves the image, sound, and stage persona of vintage Queen. Audiences are amazed at the accuracy of Queen Nation’s live retrospective journey through such Queen songs as We Will Rock You – Bohemian Rhapsody – We Are The Champions – Crazy Little Thing Called Love – You’re My Best Friend – Another One Bites The Dust – Under Pressure and many more. They have performed in front of thousands of people at casinos, fairs, festivals and private affairs. For more info visit www.queennation.com....

events.description: Registration for Park City Ski & Snowboard’s annual 4th of July 5K is now open! This popular event has been an annual tradition since the early 1980’s and is a great way to support local young athletes as you kick off your 4th of July celebration! Race registration is $30 for kids (11 and under) and $50 for adults (12 and over), and closes on July 3rd at 5:00 pm. Be sure to register early as we always sell out at our maximum of 1,000 runners. **THERE IS NO “DAY OF” REGISTRATION!!** After you register, just pick up your race number and t-shirt at Cole Sport (1615 Park Avenue in Park City) between June 30-July 3 anytime between 10:00am-5:00pm each day. There is also a limited window to pick up these items on race day from 6:30-7:30am. The course map can be found here for your convenience. events.title: Music Elevated State Tour
events.description: We’re bringing a symphonic celebration of America’s 250th birthday to Utah’s Little Switzerland! Enjoy a performance by our musicians against green backdrops and crisp alpine air, right in the heart of Midway’s Town Square. The program has a patriotic flair, featuring favorite classical works, Broadway selections, American folk music, and more, including: - Works from celebrated American composers like Aaron Copland and Leroy Anderson, including John Williams’ Liberty Fanfare. Western-inspired pieces \(perfect for iconic Western vistas\)—Elie Siegmeister’s Western Suite and Aaron Copland’s “Hoe-Down” from Rodeo. Western Suite was first recorded with the Utah Symphony under Maurice Abravanel in 1971. - Iconic classical works like the beautiful Adagietto from Mahler’s Symphony No. 5, a Maurice Abravanel favorite and first recorded by the Utah Symphony in 1975. - Performances by violinist Aubree Oliverson, a Utah native and recent Masterworks soloist for the Utah Symphony. - Soprano Amy Owens \(former Utah Opera Resident Artist\) performs “Glitter and Be Gay” from Bernstein’s Candide, as well as “Quando m’en vo” from Puccini’s La bohème and Ricky Ian Gordon’s “Simple Child” from The Grapes of Wrath. These pieces honor Utah’s world-class arts legacy: La bohème was Utah Opera’s first production in 1978 and The Grapes of Wrath was co-commissioned and premiered in 2007...
events.location: Midway Music on the Square, Midway, UT

events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....
events.location: Glenwood Cemetery, Park City, UT

events.title: intro to embroidery
events.description: intro to embroidery Tuesday, July 7th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required In this embroidery class, students will learn foundational hand embroidery techniques and practice a variety of basic stitches using original patterns designed by the instructor. Participants will become familiar with tools and materials, including embroidery hoops, floss, needles, and fabric. Open to all skill levels, the class offers a relaxing and creative environment that encourages mindfulness and self-expression through fiber art. This event is for 12 and up. Ally Rocks is a Salt Lake City-based artist with a passion for embroidery, sewing, and graphic design. Inspired by reptiles, motorcycles, and travel, her work reflects a love of creativity and exploration. She holds a BA in Art History from New Mexico State University and an MA in Design from Joshibi University of Art and Design, where she studied as a recipient of the MEXT Scholarship Program. Her time living and studying in Japan continues to influence her artistic perspective and practice. Check out all her work at Gaikotsudesign.com and on Instagram @gaikotsu\_design ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.title: Opera on the Patio: Debussy & The Schumanns
events.description: On July 8, Opera on the Patio pairs the intimate, emotional ly direct songs of Robert Schumann and Clara Schumann with the luminous, color-rich world of Claude Debussy. These composers changed how music feels and moves - and we invite you to explore their works with us! Back for its third season, Opera on the Patio is a free outdoor performance at the Park City Library. Bring a picnic, your family, and even your dog for an evening of beloved opera set against the backdrop of a summer sunset. Enjoy great music in a relaxed, welcoming setting \(no tickets required\). Check back soon for more details, including the concert program, performer biographies, and more!...

events.description: Join Professor **Olivier Peronnet** as your guide to sipping like the French do; elegantly and yes, often before noon! Explore food-friendly wines that pair perfectly with brunch, and long, lazy conversations. Add some baguettes and cheese and you're well into the afternoon before ya know it. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. Don't we all deserve a little recess time?...

events.title: Allen Stone
events.description: Grammy-nominated artist Allen Stone delivers an unforgettable fusion of soul, funk, and feel-good energy. Allen Stone, a soul/R&B luminary, has enraptured global audiences with his electrifying performances and soulful melodies, embodying the essence of classic soul legends. His genre-defying style, blending funk, gospel, folk-rock, and soul, has garnered critical acclaim and a devoted following. Stone's journey to musical stardom commenced in Chewelah, Washington, where his upbringing immersed him in gospel music as a pastor's son. Relocating to Seattle, he tirelessly toured the West Coast in his '87 Buick, swiftly gaining acclaim for his dynamic live shows, often playing up to 200 dates annually alongside luminaries such as Stevie Wonder, Al Green, Dave Matthews Band, Erykah Badu, and Gary Clark Jr. Notably, Stone recently hit the road with Chris Stapleton, further solidifying his status as a tour de force in the music industry. Alongside his prolific touring schedule, Stone has graced national television screens and mentored on ABC's American Idol, underscoring his esteemed position in the music realm. Stone continues to garner critical acclaim with his latest body of work, Mystery, featuring the title track in collaboration with PJ Morton. This release brings infectious rhythms and emotional depth, solidifying Stone’s place as a powerful voice in modern soul music. Opening support Wyatt Pike is an indie-folk songwriter known for crafting story-driven songs filled with vivid imagery, emotional honesty, and reflections on real-life experiences. He first gained attention with his debut EP, Brand New Boy, and most recently released the acoustic project Face The Weather. His music explores the journey of personal growth and self-discovery. As Alt Recess noted, his work represents "the next chapter—more assured, more grounded, but still brimming with the tender honesty that put him on the map." His upcoming 2026 releases will continue to build on that authentic and

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days 2026 brings together a Native Market, Hoop Competition, and Indigenous Fashion Show for Native and non-Native communities in Park City. The event will take place July 10-11, 2026 at Canyons Village, with the Indigenous Art Market held in the Forum Tent, the Hoop Contest taking place at Canyons Village, and the Indigenous Fashion Show scheduled for Saturday, July 11 at 6:30 PM in the Grand Summit Ballroom. The event celebrates Native artists, performers, designers, and community members through art, dance, fashion, storytelling, an d cultural engagement. The Native Market creates an opportunity for visitors to appreciate and support Native American artists and businesses, while the Hoop Competition honors Indigenous storytelling and tradition through dance. The Fashion Show will feature Indigenous designers and models sharing contemporary Native fashion on the runway in Park City. Native Market Days is an opportunity to bring Native voices, creativity, and presence into one of Utah's most visible destination communities while supporting American Indian Services' broader mission of Native education, opportunity, and community connection....
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Sarah Glover Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 11 and 12, Chef Sarah Glover brings her signature, land-driven approach to the flame, preparing locally sourced game, fresh-baked breads, and seasonal ingredients over open fire. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: Chef Sarah Glover Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 11 and 12, Chef Sarah Glover brings her signature, land-driven approach to the flame, preparing locally sourced game, fresh-baked breads, and seasonal ingredients over open fire. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: Cacao Workshop
events.description: **Cacao Workshop** Presented by: Esmeralda Torres and the Lopez Aquino Family of Chiapas, Mexico, and Utah Experience a cultural immersion into the Zoque-Maya chocolate-making tradition! Participants will learn the origin and cultural significance of chocolate, use traditional tools to grind cacao beans from farms in Chiapas into chocolate using hands-on processes, and taste traditional chocolate beverages, chocolates, and other recipes. This workshop offers space to reflect on the profound depth of ancestral knowledge and earth-sustaining practices, while examining the vibrant parallels within Utah's diverse communities, where generational wisdom is honored preserving ancestral recipes and traditional food preparation methods. Class includes all materials and ingredients. Ages 12 and up. $30. Scholarships available. Please email Team@summitcommunitygardens.org for more information. **Taller de Cacao** Presentado por: Esmeralda Torres y la familia López Aquino, de Chiapas (México) y Utah ¡Viva una inmersión cultural en la tradición chocolatera zoque-maya! Los participantes aprenderán sobre el origen y el significado cultural del chocolate, utilizarán herramientas tradicionales para moler granos de cacao —provenientes de las fincas en Chiapas— y transformarlos en chocolate mediante procesos prácticos, además de degustar bebidas de chocolate tradicionales, chocolates y otras recetas. Este taller ofrece un espacio para reflexionar sobre la profunda riqueza del conocimiento ancestral y las prácticas de sostenibilidad ecológica, al tiempo que examina los vibrantes paralelismos existentes en las diversas comunidades de Utah, donde se honra la sabiduría generacional mediante la preservación de recetas ancestrales y métodos tradicionales de preparación de alimentos. La clase incluye todos los materiales e ingredientes. A partir de los 12 años. $30. Becas disponibles. Escriba a Team@summitcommunitygardens.org para más información....
events.location: Summit Community Gardens and EATS, 4056 Shadow Mountain Dr, Park City, UT, 84098

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Sisters of the Holy Cross lecture part 2 by David Nicholas & Stuart Stanek
events.description: Park City Museum will host a lecture called *Sisters of the Holy Cross Part 2* by David Nicholas and Stuart Stanek on Wednesday, July 15, 2026 from 5-6 p.m. held at the Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive. More information can be found at www.parkcityhistory.org. This presentation follows their November 12, 2025 lecture on the Sisters of the Holy Cross and the Order’s 146 years of service to underserved communities in Utah and Park City. The evening’s discussion will focus on the Sisters’ controversial efforts between approximately 1980 and 1994 to expand access to health care in a community where medical services were otherwise limited to Salt Lake City. Topics will include their management of the Park City Ambulance Corps, plans to establish a hospital in Kimball Junction, and their heroic response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic at a time when other regional medical institutions failed to address the growing crisis. David Nicholas serves on the Board of the Park City Museum, is interested in local history, and frequently contributes to the *Way We Were* series. Also, he conducts periodic lectures and historic hikes. His particular interests include but are not limited to Park City’s transportation history and local plane crashes. Stuart Stanek is a forty-year broadcasting veteran. He managed one hundred and twenty-five radio stations in twenty-five different markets across the country. He served as a general manager and a regional President for iHeart Radio which is the nation’s largest broadcaster. He was involved in all facets of the business including sales, programming, operations, and marketing. He is married to Ann Crooks and they have lived in Park City for thirty-five years....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....

events.title: Raising Voices En Pointe
events.description: RAISING VOICES EN POINTE - Free film screening \+ Q&A Ballet lovers hold deep reverence for classics like The Nutcracker and La Bayadère, and for decades many fans overlooked the racist Asian caricatures embedded in some of these productions. When ballet dancers Phil Chan \(resident choreographer at the Oakland Ballet Company\) and Georgina Pazcoguin \(first female AAPI soloist at the New York City Ballet and recent Ballet West guest artist\) launched the Final Bow for Yellow Face movement, they were met with pushback from traditionalists who accused them of trying to change history. Post-film discussion and reception with Ballet West Artistic Director Adam Sklute and Phil Chan, co-founder of Final Bow for Yellowface. Part of the Ballet West Summit Festival July 15-17....

events.description: Professor Laura Wheatley will present a class on the celebration of peak-season produce with wines that bring out the best in garden-fresh ingredients. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. events.title: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — If there’s one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it’s Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the “Ode to Joy,” as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner—an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven’s Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season. July 17 | Gates Open: 6:30 p.m. | Show Time: 8 p.m. July 18 | Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.

events.description: Ballet West presents Ballet West at the Summit July 17, 2026 - 7 PM Canyons Village Amphitheater 4000 Canyons Resort Drive Park City, UT 84098 Single tickets on sale: 4/8/2026 Doors open 5:30 PM Ballet West Principal Dancers Katlyn Addison, Jenna Rae Herrera, Hadriel Diniz, and David Huffmire performing three lively works: Q.V.C. by Lou Christensen, L'air d'Esprit by Gerard Aripino, and After the Rain Pas de Deux by Christopher Wheeldon. Includes an artÉmotion Choreographic Workshop performance featuring students from the Frederick Quinney Lawson Ballet West Academy performing world premieres by internationally renowned choreographers Phil Chan, Ingrid Silva, Garrett Smith, and Rex Tilton. Run time: approximately 2 hours including intermission \(subject to change\) Recommended for ages 8 and up. No babes in arms. All patrons must have a ticket regardless of age. Portable chairs of any height allowed, there is a designated section for low-height \(9″ or less from ground\) and standard-height chairs. Blankets and picnic baskets/charcuterie/food are welcome \(no alcoholic beverages allowed\), no glass containers, beverages must be in factory-sealed plastic containers 32oz or less, empty reuseable bottles allowed 32oz or less and water stations available. All bags may be subject to search. Free parking available but paid hotel parking also available. ADA seating available. Only certified service animals allowed in venue, emotional support/other animals must stay at home....

events.title: Kenneth Peloke: Year of the Horse
events.description: Working in oil on large-format canvases, Kenneth Peloke approaches the horse not as subject but as a vessel for energy, instinct, and the kind of presence that resists easy translation into paint. His process is physical and deliberate, built through layers of mark-making that accumulate into something that feels less rendered than conjured. Year of the Horse brings together new works that explore what the animal has always represented in the human imagination: freedom, strength, the untamed thing we are drawn to and can never fully possess. Join us July 18th to experience new works by Kenneth Peloke at Gallery MAR, Park City....

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." GorgeousGourds.net...

events.title: wasatch wildflower festival
events.description: Sunday, July 19th | 9am-2pm | Alta Ski Area | Free | Registration is Required Held annually, the Wasatch Wildflower Festival is a multi-day event hosted at the four Cottonwood Canyons ski areas to celebrate the beauty and diversity of wildflowers in the Wasatch Mountains. The 2026 Festival will be held on the following dates and locations: July 11 - Brighton Ski Resort July 12 - Solitude Mountain Resort July 18 - Snowbird Ski Resort July 19 - Alta Ski Area The Festival begins at 9 am each day, and the last guided walk leaves at 1 pm. The event is free, but registration is required. Registration is now open! Founded in 1996 by a group of Alta locals and Alta Community Enrichment \(one of their first events\), the Festival is a special opportunity to educate visitors on how to ensure the protection of this important and unique natural resource. The Wasatch Wildflower Festival's guided walks and activities are designed to be fun, family-friendly, non-commercial, free of charge, and open to all interests and ability levels. Volunteers share their knowledge and enthusiasm for an amazing variety of species on a variety of beautiful walks. The tours also reinforce careful stewardship and awareness of the ecology of the watershed environment. Come enjoy multiple days of wildflower walks, kids' crafts, music, food, and a healthy dose of the beautiful Cottonwood Canyons. The 2025 Festival is cohosted with the Brighton Institute at Brighton and Solitude. Snowbird Sustainability, and Alta Community Enrichment and Alta Environmental Center, are cohosting at Snowbird and Alta, respectively....
events.location: Alta Ski Area, Alta, UT

events.title: Volunteer Dig Day July 19th - Anchor Trees
events.description: Greetings Volunteers! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. We'll be meeting at the private parking area directly west of the Bonanza Flat parking lot at 8:30 am \(no fee to park\). We'll then head out to do dirt work on the new hiking trail connecting 9k and Mid Mountain. If you're a mountain biker wondering why you should be doing work on a hike-only trail, please remember that these options alleviate traffic and help to optimize bike-only options throughout the system. We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. This project presents an excellent opportunity to create a unique legacy trail that will benefit you and the community for years to come! Timeframe: 8:30-8:45 Meet at the trailhead 8:45 Head to worksite 12:00 Return to trailhead Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Mozart’s “Jupiter”
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart’s second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. Framing the concerto are Mozart’s two final symphonies—written just weeks apart—revealing the fascinating journey of the composer's musical maturation and his unmistakable brilliance. One of Mozart’s most recognizable works, his Symphony No. 40 uses a minor key—a rare choice for the composer—to build emotional intensity and dark lyricism. In contrast, the program concludes with the joyful Symphony No. 41, whose grandeur and musical tour de force ultimately earned it the nickname “Jupiter,” honoring the Roman god....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....

events.title: 38 Special
events.description: 38 Special – Live in Concert Bring on the anthems. 38 Special delivers high-energy, Southern-fried rock that defined an era and still packs the house decades later. Known for blending catchy hooks with driving guitar riffs, the band has become a staple of American rock radio and live performance. With iconic hits like “Hold On Loosely,” “Caught Up in You,” and “Rockin’ Into the Night,” 38 Special has built a legacy of feel-good, sing-along songs that cross generations. Their live shows are tight, powerful, and packed with the kind of rock-and-roll energy that turns a concert into a shared memory. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this is a show made for fans of classic rock, summer nights, and timeless music that still hits just right. Fox Brothers Band Get ready for an unforgettable night of high-energy entertainment with the Fox Brothers Band, one of Utah’s most sought-after live music acts. Blending the best of Country, Rock, and Pop, the band delivers a dynamic performance packed with crowd favorites, modern hits, and timeless classics that keep audiences singing and dancing all night long. Known for their infectious energy, exceptional musicianship, and engaging stage presence, the Fox Brothers Band has built a reputation for creating memorable live experiences for audiences of all ages. The band's members have individually performed with or opened for nationally recognized artists including Chris Janson, Riley Green, Chase Rice, Ian Munsick, Brantley Gilbert, Neon Trees, and many others.

All races start and finish on the Plaza Deck at the Snowbird Center, Entry 2. The race village is located in the Upper Circle parking lot outside the Snowbird Center. Parking is available for participants, friends, family and spectators in Snowbird's main lots. events.title: Taylor Dayne
events.description: Taylor Dayne Taylor Dayne is an American pop icon. With a career spanning three decades, Taylor’s Groundbreaking debut single “Tell It To My Heart” turned her into an overnight international star in 1987. She followed the smash hit with seventeen Top 20 singles over the course of her three-decade career, including Number Ones “Love Will Lead You Back”, “Prove Your Love”, and “I’ll Always Love You.” Taylor has sold over 75 million albums and singles worldwide, earned three GRAMMY nominations, an American Music Award, multiple New York Music Awards, received New York Music Hall of Fame honors and ranked as the Number 18 Female Dance Artist of all time by Rolling Stone magazine. She is one of the very few artists in musical history to successfully crossover into almost every musical genre and chart with hit singles in Pop, Dance, R&B, Adult contemporary, and Rock. Exposé Get ready for a night of unforgettable 80s and 90s dance-pop when Exposé takes the stage. This legendary female vocal group from Miami helped define the freestyle and dance-pop sound of the late 1980s and early 1990s, delivering a string of iconic hits that still fill dance floors today. With powerhouse vocals and timeless melodies, Exposé became the first group ever to score four Top 10 hits from their debut album, including classics like “Come Go With Me,” “Point of No Return,” “Let Me Be the One,” and their No. 1 smash hit “Seasons Change.” Featuring the celebrated voices of Jeanette Jurado, Ann Curless, and Gioia Bruno, Exposé continues to tour and delight fans worldwide with their signature blend of high-energy dance anthems and heartfelt ballads. From chart-topping radio hits to beloved freestyle club favorites, Exposé delivers a show packed with nostalgia, powerful vocals, and infectious rhythms that will have audiences singing along all night. Join us for an electrifying evening celebrating one of the most successful girl groups of the 80s and a sound that defined a generation.

events.title: Stravinsky's Firebird Suite & String Trio "Time for Three
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite takes flight in this spectacular program led by Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner. First, GRAMMY®-winning trio Time for Three—known for their genre-defying blend of classical, Americana, and contemporary styles— takes the stage for Kevin Puts’ Contact, the winner of a GRAMMY® for Best Contemporary Classical Composition. Written specifically for the group by the Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, the work explores themes of human connection and highlights the trio’s dynamic, longtime partnership with the Utah Symphony. Then, hear one of the most thrilling pieces ever written echoing through the Wasatch Mountains as the orchestra takes you on a journey from the explosive “Infernal Dance” to the powerful finale of Stravinsky’s Firebird. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Best Day Ever
events.description: “Best Day Ever” follows adaptive mountain bikers Greg Durso and Allie Bianchi as they tackle the daily challenges of disability-and find joy, connection, and belonging in Vermont’s mountain biking community. Set in the Green Mountains, this is a story of grit, independence, and what’s possible when community and creativity come together. At its heart is the world’s first fully adaptive trail network—built to prove that everyone belongs outside. Visually stunning and emotionally powerful, “Best Day Ever” challenges perceptions of disability and celebrates community. Post-film Q&A with Greg Durso. Reception to follow in Community Room. Presented as part of the Raising Voices Film Series in partnership with the Kelly Brush Foundation. Ticket proceeds benefit the Kelly Brush Foundation....

events.title: Overland Cookery Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. Led by Overland Cookery’s expedition chefs, this weekend draws inspiration from cooking in remote environments. Open fire techniques and thoughtful preparation create an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, it is an easy, social setting to spend the afternoon, where food, fire, and the outdoors come together. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.description: “We don’t have a lot of love songs. We have life songs.” – Mark Miller For more than 40 years, Sawyer Brown has delivered high-energy, blue-collar country-rock that keeps audiences on their feet. From winning Star Search to sharing stages with legends like Kenny Rogers and Dolly Parton, they’ve built a career defined by nonstop touring and unforgettable live shows. With 51 charted singles—including the #1 hit “Some Girls Do”—and over 6,000 performances worldwide, Sawyer Brown continues to bring the same grassroots spirit and electrifying presence that made them a household name. Skylar Geer Experience an evening of authentic country music with Utah singer-songwriter Skylar Geer, a talented performer known for his rich vocals, heartfelt songwriting, and energetic live shows. Blending traditional country roots with Southern rock influences, Skylar creates a sound that is both timeless and fresh, connecting with audiences through stories of life, love, and the American West. After spending several years in Nashville honing his craft as a songwriter and performer, Skylar returned to Utah and has become a favorite on stages throughout the Mountain West. Backed by a talented band featuring fiddle, guitars, bass, and drums, his performances seamlessly mix original music with crowd-favorite country classics and modern hits, all delivered with his signature style.
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Overland Cookery Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. Led by Overland Cookery’s expedition chefs, this weekend draws inspiration from cooking in remote environments. Open fire techniques and thoughtful preparation create an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, it is an easy, social setting to spend the afternoon, where food, fire, and the outdoors come together. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: Volunteer Dig Day July 28th - Anchor Trees
events.description: Greetings Volunteers! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. We'll be meeting at the private parking area directly west of the Bonanza Flat parking lot at 5pm \(no fee to park\). We'll then head out to do dirt work on the new hiking trail connecting 9k and Mid Mountain. If you're a mountain biker wondering why you should be doing work on a hike-only trail, please remember that these options alleviate traffic and help to optimize bike-only options throughout the system. We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. This project presents an excellent opportunity to create a unique legacy trail that will benefit you and the community for years to come! Timeframe: 5:00pm Meet at the trailhead 5:15pm Head to worksite 8:00pm Return to trailhead Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Shakespeare’s Legacy: From the Globe to Grand Opera lecture by Ben Beckman
events.description: Park City Museum will host a lecture called *Shakespeare’s Legacy: From the Globe to Grand Opera* by Ben Beckman on Wednesday, July 29, 2026 from 5-6 p.m. held at the Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive. More information can be found at www.parkcityhistory.org. William Shakespeare’s influence extends far beyond the stage: over 400 years after his death, his plays continue to be read and performed around the world, and adaptations of his work in film, television, musical theater, and more abound. The Bard also leaves a deep legacy in the field of opera, inspiring favorites in the operatic canon by composers like Verdi, Berlioz, Purcell, Barber – and Gounod. Ahead of Park City Opera’s production of Gounod’s *Roméo et Juliette*, join us to explore Shakespeare’s enduring legacy in opera, the history of Shakespeare performance in Utah, and the story behind Gounod’s masterpiece. Benjamin Beckman is a composer, conductor, and pianist who serves as the Artistic Director of Park City Opera. He studied at Yale and the University of Southern California, and has worked with opera companies around America, including The Atlanta Opera, Sarasota Opera, Festival Napa Valley, Pacific Opera Project, The Opera Guild of Palm Springs, Opera Company of Middlebury Vermont, and Chicago Summer Opera. He will be conducting Park City Opera's production of Gounod’s *Roméo et Juliette* at the Eccles Center in Downtown Park City on August 21st and 23rd....
events.location: Education and Collections Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.

events.title: Psychedelic Furs with Special Guest Living Colour
events.description: The Psychedelic Furs may not have invented rock & roll per se, but their influence since arriving on the post-punk scorched-earth landscape four decades ago has reverberated and resonated among all those who cherish the sweet-and-sour spot where rawness and romanticism meet. Born out of the UK post-punk scene and led by the vocalist and songwriter Richard Butler, and his bass-wielding brother Tim, the Furs quickly developed as one of the premiere bands at college and alternative radio, scoring a flurry of major hits with "Love My Way," "Pretty In Pink," "Heaven," "The Ghost In You, “And “Heartbreak Beat” in all releasing eight studio albums, spawning several compilations, a boxed set, a live concert DVD, and inspiring one of the most iconic motion picture soundtracks of all time. Decades later, their latest release, “Made Of Rain” charted in a multitude of countries and became the Furs’ Second-highest-ranked UK Album ever. It was prominently featured at the end of the year “Best Albums” chart roundup in dozens of publications worldwide. But the Furs especially thrive live in concert, having headlined at the U.K.’s famed Glastonbury Festival, and more recently at esteemed venues including the Hollywood Bowl, London’s Royal Albert Hall, New York’s Radio City Music Hall,and Nashville’s Grand ‘le Opry, among others. With a dazzling live show, they continue to tour quite regularly across the globe Living Colour Few bands have reshaped the sound and spirit of rock music like Living Colour. Formed in New York City in 1984 and led by visionary guitarist Vernon Reid, the band—Corey Glover \(vocals\), Will Calhoun \(drums\), and Doug Wimbish \(bass\)—delivers a powerful fusion of rock, funk, jazz, and metal that remains as bold and relevant today as ever. Living Colour burst onto the scene with their groundbreaking debut album Vivid \(1988\), which climbed to \#6 on the Billboard 200 and earned double-platinum status. The album featured the iconic anthem “Cult of Perso...

events.title: Boney James
events.description: Saxophonist, Producer and Composer Boney James’ New Album ‘Slow Burn’ slow burn: noun, informal A feeling that grows with a slow but deliberate intensity. For saxophonist and composer Boney James, these words perfectly describe the music he has crafted for his new Concord Records album, Slow Burn, to be released on October 18, 2024. “When I mentioned that phrase to people as a possible title, they said, “That also refers to your whole career in a way” James says with a laugh. “This is my 19th record and I feel like I’m just hitting my stride.” Slow Burn is a new high point in a remarkable career that now spans more than three decades and includes a long list of accolades; four GRAMMY nominations, three NAACP Image Award nominations, a Soul Train Award for Best Jazz Album and four RIAA Gold Record certifications. In 2009, Billboard named James one of the Top 3 Contemporary Jazz Artists of the decade. In 2024 year Boney became the first ever artist to score 20 number one singles on the Billboard Smooth Jazz chart. But Boney James has never been one to look backwards and Slow Burn, which the artist produced, marks several firsts. Beginning with the album’s opening track, ‘Arcadia.’ The very first notes on the album are the acoustic bass playing of the legendary Marcus Miller. It’s one of the few times that Miller, known for his electric bass guitar work, has committed his standup bass playing to a recording and the first time that James has incorporated an upright bass into his own music Next is James’ intriguing interpretation of the classic ‘Butterfly.’ Co-written by master keyboardist Herbie Hancock and Bennie Maupin, the track features Marcus Miller again as well as young lion Cory Henry’s inspired electric piano. “This beautiful melody got stuck in my head” explains James. “I have never covered a tune by a legend like Herbie before and I hope we did him justice.” The title track, ‘Slow Burn,’ is one of four tunes on the album that James co-wrote an

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Reception & Five-Course Dinner
events.description: The Taste of Luxury Series featuring Stag's Leap — Deer Valley’s Taste of Luxury Series continues with a celebration honoring the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Judgment of Paris, the landmark tasting that forever changed the global perception of California wine and established Napa Valley as a world-class wine region. To commemorate the occasion, Deer Valley welcomes Marcus Notaro, head winemaker of Stag's Leap Wine Cellars, alongside returning guest Chef Clement Gelas of Talisker Club for an intimate evening at Deer Valley Café. Set pond-side at sunset, guests will enjoy a thoughtfully curated five-course pairing experience inspired by the wines, stories, and legacy behind this groundbreaking moment in wine history. RESERVE YOUR TICKETS EVENT SCHEDULE Meet-and-Greet Reception 6:30 p.m. Five-Course Dinner & Wine Pairing 7 p.m.

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 1 and 2, Chef Ryan Arensdorf of KC Que takes the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Chef Peter Menteer prepares paella in an 8-foot pan with local meats and fresh seafood, alongside Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon cooked over open fire pits. Local Utah breweries and live entertainment at Silver Lake Village round out the experience. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 1 and 2, Chef Ryan Arensdorf of KC Que takes the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Chef Peter Menteer prepares paella in an 8-foot pan with local meats and fresh seafood, alongside Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon cooked over open fire pits. Local Utah breweries and live entertainment at Silver Lake Village round out the experience. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: movies under the stars: the road to patagonia
events.description: MOVIES UNDER THE STARS: the road to patagonia Tuesday, August 4th | Gates 7:30 pm, LIVE MUSIC BY JIM FISH STARTING AT 7:30 pm | Movie at 8:30 pm | Alta Peruvian Back Lawn│$15 | Tickets | Kids Under 5 are FREE THE ROAD TO PATAGONIA Is a stunning, intimate and unflinching series of love letters within a documentary - firstly, a love between two people, and secondly between humanity and the Earth. Ecologist Matty Hannon begins an incredible solo adventure, to surf the west coast of the Americas by motorbike, from the top of Alaska to the tip of Patagonia. But deep in the wilderness - alone with the wolves and the bears - the journeyer's plans unexpectedly fall to pieces. After losing everything, and on the cusp of quitting, he meets the girl of his dreams, a permaculture farmer named Heather. Shot over 16 years, the result is an adventurous exposé on the more-than-human-world, offering a physical and spiritual odyssey to better understand our place in Nature. Winner - Best Film and Best Documentary - Byron Bay International Film Festival Winner Audience Choice Award - Florida Surf Film Festival Winner Audience Choice Award - Melbourne Documentary Film Festival FACTS & TIPS: You must bring your own LOW chairs and they must be 31.5" height or less. Or blankets to sit on. Picnic dinners are allowed. NO GRILLS. If two separate households would like to be near each other we recommend arriving at the same time. No dogs, unless tagged by the Town of Alta or an ADA service dog. Prepare for chilly mountain evenings, bring blankets and layers. If you have questions regarding accessibility to our events, please contact Sara Gibbs, Executive Director. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...
events.location: Alta Peruvian Lawn 10000 East Highway 210, Alta, UT
